key_insight: CANN-EUCLID discovers sparse, interpretable hyperelastic constitutive laws directly from full-field displacement and force data, without local stress measurements or a prescribed model.
engineering_relevance: It enables material model identification from a single heterogeneous test, which is especially valuable for soft tissues and other specimens where repeated homogeneous testing is impractical or damaging.
method: Combine constitutive neural networks with the EUCLID full-field, stress-unsupervised framework.
Minimize equilibrium imbalance while using sparsity regularization to select compact active terms.
Identify isotropic and anisotropic hyperelastic laws from displacement fields and reaction forces.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks show near-exact recovery when the true law is within the CANN basis, and forward FE validation reproduces ground-truth behavior.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on the deformation states sampled; if key stiffening regimes are not probed, extrapolation can be poor.
paper_title: CANN-EUCLID: unsupervised constitutive artificial neural network model discovery from full-field data
